title: SMBv1 Enabled with Signing Disabled
description: SMBv1 protocol is enabled and SMB signing is not required. SMBv1 is vulnerable to multiple critical exploits (EternalBlue, etc.). Without SMB signing, the server is vulnerable to relay attacks. Additionally, null session enumeration revealed 35 system users and the hostname 'METASPLOITABLE'.
evidence:
SMB Configuration Analysis:
- SMBv1 protocol is enabled (critical)
- SMB signing is not required (high)

SMB Null Session Enumeration:
- Domain: localdomain
- OS: METASPLOITABLE
- Users enumerated (35): root, msfadmin, user, service, postgres, mysql, tomcat55, distccd, proftpd, ftp, www-data, sshd, telnetd, backup, daemon, sys, bin, mail, games, nobody, proxy, syslog, news, lp, man, uucp, gnats, libuuid, dhcp, klog, postfix, irc, list, sync, bind
remediation: Disable SMBv1 protocol. Enable and require SMB signing. Restrict null session access. Upgrade Samba to a current, supported version.

issues: HIGH severity finding should have stronger evidence
Exploitation proof required for high/critical findings
suggestions: Add command output demonstrating access or data extraction
For high findings, demonstrate actual impact
Actually exploit the vulnerability and capture output
